title: Google Health has fixed the outage that broke sync for Fitbit & Pixel Watch owners [U]
summary: A widespread outage on July 15 has broken device sync for “all” Google Health users.

title: The Oura Ring 5 is the first smart ring to truly fit my routine and feel like jewelry I’d buy
summary: Comfort is key when it comes to passive tracking.
body: For the past few years, recommending a smart ring has been fairly easy. If someone asked me which one to buy, I almost always pointed them toward Oura. The only hiccup was deciding whether the ring’s premium price and ongoing subscription were worth it. In other words, some wearable lines need a reinvention to stay relevant, but the Oura Ring wasn’t one of them. Rather than chasing a flashy new health metric or gimmicky feature, Oura spent this generation refining what already worked and delivered a dramatically smaller device without sacrificing accuracy or battery life. The story isn’t just about what the Oura Ring 5 () can do (which is a lot), but how much easier it is to wear around the clock.

title: Is the Google Health app down for you? Here’s what’s going on (Update: Fixed)
summary: Is Google Health down for you?
body: Credit: Google TL;DR The Google Health app is experiencing issues. Users are having trouble with syncing and pairing. The issues appear to have started around 7:00 AM ET. Update: July 15, 2026 (4:22 PM ET): According to the status page for Google Health, the syncing/pairing issue has been fixed. Original article: July 15, 2026 (9:58 AM ET): If you’re having trouble with the Google Health app, know that you’re not the only one. It appears that Google’s fitness app is currently experiencing issues.

title: Google Health 5.03 rolls out: More Today stats, naps part of sleep total [U]
summary: Google is rolling out version 5.03 of the Health app with more quality-of-life updates on Android and iOS.

title: Samsung officially confirms everything new on Galaxy Watch 9 in teaser [Video]
summary: Ahead of its debut barely a week from now, Samsung is officially teasing the Galaxy Watch 9 with its returning circle-on-a-square design, new health insights, and an “all-new inside.”
